description: A nutritional scientist is reviewing the classic ToothGrowth experiment (Crampton 1947) studying the effect of Vitamin C delivery method on guinea pig tooth growth. Using the 'ToothGrowth.csv' dataset already loaded in Jamovi, compare orange juice (OJ) vs ascorbic acid (VC) supplements at the 0.5 mg/day dose level only. Filter the data to include only the 0.5 mg dose level and perform an Independent Samples T-Test with 'len' as the dependent variable and 'supp' as the grouping variable, ensuring descriptive statistics are included in the analysis. Save the Jamovi project to /home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/ToothGrowth_Filtered.omv. Create a plain text results file at /home/ga/Documents/Jamovi/filtered_ttest_results.txt containing the following key-value pairs (one per line, colon-separated): n_filtered, t_statistic, p_value, mean_OJ, and mean_VC.
